title: dysphania multiflora
intro: Dysphania multiflora, also known as Chenopodium multiflorum, is a species of flowering plant in the amaranth family that is native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Dysphania multiflora include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Dysphania multiflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine in the form of a poultice or salve.
